video_name: Video_03_Introduction to LangChain ｜ LangChain for Beginners ｜ Video 1 ｜ CampusX.mp3
full_text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel So this is going to be a very exciting video because today we are going to start a new playlist and the name of this playlist is Generative AI using Langchain I had talked about this playlist in detail in the last video and I am pretty sure you are very excited for this playlist and in today's video we are not going to do too much practical things but what I will do is that I will take you to a very fundamental level and I will try to explain what is Langchain and more importantly why Langchain is needed In this video we will discuss what you can do using Langchain and at the end I will tell you what are the other popular alternatives of Langchain. On the whole this video will set up a very beautiful theoretical base for the next videos so that is why this video is super important Rest I am super excited for this playlist I really hope you are also there. Let's start this video So let's start our discussion around Langchain The first and most obvious question is What is Langchain? What is Langchain? So I would like to give you the answer to this question through this single sentence So Langchain is an open source framework for developing applications powered by LLMs So if you want to make an LLM based application then the framework that helps you in making that application is Langchain Now with this single sentence you will not understand the importance of Langchain I truly feel that whenever you want to read anything, you should first understand why that thing was needed If you want to study what is X, you should know why X was needed in the first place So now I am going to explain what Langchain is I will first explain why Langchain was needed So let's move on to the next slide and let's discuss why do we need Langchain and why we need this framework and trust me I will take a little longer to prove this point but trust me when this discussion ends you will have a very deep perspective and you will realize how important Langchain is So I am going to give you an example from my own life So I have been working in the world of startups and I have done multiple startups myself and I have explored many ideas in the last 10 years So around 2014, I had an idea in my mind and at that time there was not much AI buzz at least in India So at that time I had an idea in my mind that we have started reading a lot of PDFs Before that people used to read a lot of books but in 2014-2015 smartphones became prevalent so people started reading a lot of PDFs So I had an idea in my mind that what if I make an application where anyone can upload their PDFs and then can read those PDFs So it is a PDF reader and not only can it read but at the same time there will be a chat feature in our application and by clicking on that chat feature you can talk to your PDFs For example, suppose I have uploaded a machine learning book and in this book the entire machine learning is covered So what can I do? Not only can I read this book but at the same time I can ask a lot of questions through the chat For example, I can ask page number 5 to be explained as I am a 5 year old child and that chatbot can give me a simplified version of page 5 Then I can ask this question that the linear regression we have read can generate some true false questions on it Or I can ask this question that the decision tree we have read in this book can generate notes on it So I hope you are understanding that this kind of application can be incredibly useful because not only you can read but at the same time you can talk to your book So in 2014, I got an idea that maybe I should work on it Now I will tell you next how I planned to build this application and what was the system design and how I was planning to execute it Let's discuss a high level how we can make an application like this Once you understand the high level then we will discuss some details So how will this system work? As soon as a user uploads a PDF in our application First we will upload that PDF and store it in our database Now the user opened that PDF and asked a query Let's say the question is What are the assumptions of linear regression? So what we have to do is first of all we have to find out where this topic has been discussed in the whole book on which pages this topic has been discussed So essentially we have to perform a search operation But there can be two types of search operations One can be normal keyword search What will you do in normal keyword search? You will catch these words like assumption is a word, linear regression is a word and you will search those words as it is in the whole book So you will search all the words in the whole book Wherever the word assumption is used or wherever the word linear regression is used we will pick up all those pages Now you can understand that this is a little inefficient We need contextual results about the assumptions of linear regression But in our case, there are many more pages Assumption word may have come in many pages But the results we are getting are a little inefficient So what we will do is rather than doing a keyword search we will do a semantic search Semantic search is a little different I am very sure you must have read it in NLP So what you do in semantic search is you try to understand the meaning of your query You will try to understand that the user needs assumptions of linear regression So you will search for assumptions of linear regression So automatically you will get those pages in the result where the assumption of linear regression is discussed So you will get less number of pages but more meaningful results So whatever it is We picked up the user's query and did a semantic search on our entire document From this we got two pages Page no. 372 and page no. 461 Assumptions of linear regression has been discussed on these two pages So we got these results here Now what we will do is we will pick up the pages in the result and the original query of the user and we will make a system query And we will send this system query to the most important component of our application which I am calling as brain for now Now this brain has one purpose Actually there are two purposes First purpose is that the user understands the meaning of the query So here NLU capability should be there Natural Language Understanding Capability should be there with our brain Which means that the user understands the query in English and in Hindi but the user should be able to understand the query So that he understands what to do Second, brain should have Context-Aware Text Generation Capability Which means Essentially what the brain has to do Is that the query is given to the user and the document of the two pages is given to him And the user is asked to understand this query So to understand this query it needs NLU capabilities And to understand and find the relevant answer and generate the answer it should get Context-Aware Text Generation So basically this brain of our system has two purposes To understand the query of the user and then we are giving it to the pages So this is Context-Aware Text Generating So what will it do? It will read the pages and then extract the five assumptions of linear regression and then generate the text and give it to us And that is our final output So this is how this entire system is working Now you must be getting some queries in your mind that why did we put so much effort So this is the semantic search If my brain understands the query of the user and can find the answer from the given pages then we can give it this entire book Suppose this book is of 1000 pages So we did so much semantic search It's better to send this 1000 page document and tell that the user asked this question Read this book and answer Imagine you are studying in a school and you get a doubt in your maths book So you are going to your teacher and you are telling him that sir, hold this book I have a doubt in algebra Now this is scenario 1, scenario 2 is that you go to your teacher and tell him specifically that sir I have a doubt in page 155 So what do you think, in which scenario So you can give this entire book Obviously in the second scenario because you gave it one single page and told that you have a doubt in this page rather than giving the entire book So same scenario is here also You gave this brain specifically 2 pages and a query of the user and told him to read this query and answer from these 2 pages Now if you give the entire book, it will be computationally So that the results you get are not that good That is why we implemented this entire feature So this is the high level overview of our system Now let's do one thing, let's dive a little deeper and understand how this system can be built in detail Now guys to understand this system at a low level we will have to understand semantic search first A little while ago I told you that In semantic search what you do is you search based on the meaning of a text So I will tell you how semantic search works So the idea is very simple Suppose you have 3 paragraphs about 3 cricketers and now you will be asked a question and you have to answer that question from these 3 paragraphs So you have to understand which paragraph has the answer hidden So how will this be done? Suppose the question is how many runs has Virat's code So we know that in the Virat Kohli paragraph the answer is hidden But how will our code understand this? So what happens is that you convert all your texts into embedding Converting into embedding means that you convert it into a vector Vector means set of numbers So essentially you want to represent the semantic meaning of this whole paragraph in the form of numbers So you can use a lot of techniques like Word2veg, Doc2veg You can generate embeddings through BERT There are a lot of techniques But the idea is that you represent this whole paragraph This is a vector Same for this paragraph and same for this paragraph Let's assume that the dimension of these vectors is 100 Basically it is a 100 dimensional vector Now we have the vectors of all 3 paragraphs Now as soon as we get this query we will generate embedding of this Basically we will give it the form of a vector So this is a vector of 100 dimension So now what happens is that I have 4 vectors in a 100 dimensional space These are the 3 vectors of your paragraphs So this is the Virat Kohli vector This is the Jaspreet Pumbra vector And this is the Rohit Sharma vector And this is the query vector And this is the query vector Now what you will do is You will get the similarity of these 3 vectors And the one with the strongest similarity You will understand that This question is related to that paragraph And then you will use this paragraph To give the answer So this is how semantic search works And we have to implement this in our system on our PDF Now that we understand how semantic search works Now I will explain you in detail the design of this system So what will happen is This whole thing will start as soon as our user uploads a PDF So after uploading the PDF We will store it in the cloud For now let's assume that we are using AWS services So we are storing that PDF in AWS S3 service Now our PDF Has come to us in the cloud Now what we have to do is Load it first So basically we need a document loader With the help of which we will bring the PDF to our system Now as soon as it comes to the system The first thing I have to do is Divide the PDF into small chunks So small chunks can be on which basis It can be on the basis of chapters, pages, even paragraphs Let's assume that we have 1000 pages PDF And we are chunking on the basis of pages So in total we have divided this whole document into 1000 parts So we are basically Using a text splitter With the help of which we are able to separate each page Now what we have to do is We have to generate embedding So here we are using an embedding model We are sending each page in this embedding model And this embedding model is generating an embedding for that page Embedding means generating a vector In a n dimensional space So now I have 1000 vectors Basically 1000 set of numbers Now what I will do is store these embeddings in a database So that I can query on them in the future We will discuss about this later These are different types of databases But whatever are the databases We are storing these embeddings in these databases Now our main work starts from here Our user came, opened the PDF and asked a query Now the query is a text So what we will do is first of all send this query To the embedding model And with that we will generate the embedding of that query Again in n dimension Now what will happen is that we already have 1000 vectors in the database Now a new vector came We will compare that vector With all the vectors Basically we will take out the distance And the set of vectors which will be closest to our query vector We will take them out Basically suppose if we decide That we have to return 5 vectors Then 5 similar vectors will come back And then we will extract the corresponding pages And we will get the same pages here I hope you understood this whole thing After that the whole flow is the same We will take the original query of the user We will take the original query and these pages And we will form our system query And this system query will go to the brain of our app And in the brain NLU plus context aware Text generation will be there With which we will get our answer And we will show the same answer in the form of final output to the user So this is the low level overview So we have discussed all the details So I really hope that The time we have spent in this video You have cleared the design of this system Now our discussion starts Now we have understood the system design of this project Now let's do a small discussion What challenges can come in building this project First of all I will talk about the biggest challenge The most challenging aspect in this Is to build the brain Think about it We have to develop a component Which we can send any query So our component can understand this query Right, now this is a very challenging task Second, after understanding It can generate relevant text Which is a very challenging task And honestly A lot of work has been done in NLP on both these things But the breakthrough that came Finally came in 2017 when the paper of transformers came And after the transformer came the papers of BERT and GPT And after that the episode of LLMs started So finally this problem was cracked So guess what We don't have to work hard to develop this brain Already LLMs are in the market Which have both these capabilities Natural language understanding capability And context aware text generating capability We simply have to use any LLM So although this was a big challenge in 2015 Today it is not a big challenge Today you can solve this problem very easily So now what I will do is In this flow, instead of this brain I will write LLM Now there are a lot of LLMs in the market You can use any open source LLM If you are a big company, you can train your own foundational model But again, whatever it is, not our concern We got the way to solve our first challenge And the way is to use LLM So now let's talk about challenge number 2 So another big challenge is That if you want to use LLM As your brain Then you have to put LLM in your system Basically you have to keep it on your server Now you may know about LLM scale That they are very heavy deep learning models Which are trained on the internet data Now if you put such a big LLM model On your servers to inference To get results from it Then you have to do a lot of engineering You have to solve a lot of computational problems Because it is not easy to keep such big systems On your server from an engineering point of view Second cost will also cost a lot So the second big challenge is of computation That how can I Bring a LLM to my cloud And run it and manage the cost So guess what, the good news is That this challenge has been solved So big companies like OpenAI Or Anthropic Or Google The companies who have made LLMs They have identified that If someone wants to use their LLM Then most likely they will not be able to run LLMs on their server or machine Because that software is Or that model is extremely heavy So what did they do? They put these LLMs on their server Like OpenAI, their chat GPT Or GPT models And they created an API around their LLM And they created an API around their LLM Now what is the benefit of this API? That anyone can talk to this LLM Simply you will ask a question That question will go to your LLM And LLM will reply to you And the reply will be returned to the user So you don't have to take the entire LLM You can simply hit these APIs And run your work So in short, I will not use LLM I will use an LLM API And the benefit of using APIs Is that I will pay as much as I use So if my usage is less, I have to reduce the payment So in short, the two big challenges Natural language understanding and text generation And the computational challenges around LLM These APIs solved them So the two big challenges have already been solved Now I will tell you the challenge number 3 In making this system Now let's talk about challenge number 3 Challenge number 3 is to orchestrate this whole system Means to be able to run all these components By mixing them So let me explain you First of all, I want to show you How many moving components are there So first of all, one component is AWS S3 Where you are storing your documents Second component is tech splitter This is also a type of model Which decides based on the document how to split Third is embedding So we do this through a model So that model is also a component And then the database where you will store your embedding Is also a component And your LLM is also a component So we have got 5 components in this system Now if I tell you about the task So in total we are performing many tasks We are performing the task of loading the document We are performing the task of splitting the text We are performing the task of embedding We are performing the task of database management We are performing the task of retrieval And we are performing the task of talking to this LLM So again 5-6 tasks are there Which we have to execute through a pipeline So in short, this system is made up of many moving components And in between those components we have to execute many tasks Which is a very challenging task If you write all this code with a scratch It could be very difficult For example, suppose you have coded this whole system with your hands And tomorrow you found out that you don't have to use OpenAI APIs because they are costly And instead you have to use the gemini API of Google So now suddenly you have to remove The entire code that you had written And you have to write a new code To interact with the gemini API of Google Similarly, instead of AWS S3 You can move to GCP Or you can use another model in the embedding model So you understand There are so many moving parts and there is a lot of interaction between them And there is a very complex workflow To be able to code all this with your hands is a very challenging task And this is where Langchain comes into the picture What Langchain does is It gives you built-in functionalities Where you can plug and play And interact all these components with each other Not only that, you don't have to use OpenAI And Google's gemini, no problem You just have to change one line of code and that's it Your job is done You don't have to write the boilerplate code for all these components Because all this work is handled by Langchain So in short, if I summarize this conversation It will be that If you want to make an LLM powered application Then a lot of heavy lifting is done by LLM But to be able to run that LLM based application With all its moving components Is a very difficult task Especially now, because technology is very new So this work becomes even more difficult And this is where Langchain comes into the picture It tells you to focus on your idea I will do the work of orchestration So now that you have a little intuition That why Langchain is being used exactly in this whole system Now I will tell you more benefits Which Langchain provides you So we tried to understand the reason behind Langchain Now I want to tell you the benefits of Langchain Formally So let's discuss one by one The first benefit of Langchain is That there is a concept of chains In fact, the name of Langchain is Langchain because it is a chain concept So what you can do in chains is You can give a chain form to different components Basically you can form a pipeline structure And you can execute many complex workflows Like in our example You have to execute multiple tasks between multiple components You have to load the document, split the text You have to do the embedding, store it in the database Then you have to do the retrieval and send the data to LLM Now this is a series of tasks So you can convert this whole pipeline into a chain So the best feature of chain is That the output of one component and the input of the other component Is automatically made You don't have to write this code manually And not only that, you can form very complex chains You can form sequential chains, parallel chains, conditional chains So no matter how complex the workflow is No matter how many tasks there are You can make that pipeline using chains So this is the biggest feature that we will see later The second big feature is Model agnostic development Meaning you can use any model It doesn't matter, I gave you an example You can use OpenAI or Google You have to change the code of one line And it will be converted You can use AWS or GCP So you can easily shift the code base To a different component So basically this model is agnostic You have to focus on your core logic, business logic Move the components there, it doesn't matter Third is that Langchain has tried to give you a complete ecosystem If you want a document loader, then all kinds of document loader Are available, you can bring the data from the cloud You can load data, excel file, PDF You can load any kind of thing You have 50 types of splitters in tech splitter You have a lot of embedding models in embedding You have a lot of databases So basically every component you use Has a lot of varieties available So whatever product you want to work with The interface is available in Langchain So you can't implement it Lastly, you get the concept of memory and state handling So for example, suppose our user queries What are the assumptions of linear regression So our system has given him the assumptions Now he immediately asked the next question Also give me a few interview questions On this machine learning algorithm But which machine learning algorithm? We don't know that last time we talked about linear regression So this problem is also solved by Langchain Here is the concept of memory Where you can use the concept of memory in conversation So if there is a talk of linear regression in a conversation So without mentioning linear regression Our model will understand that linear regression is happening And this is provided by Langchain So in short, it is a very good tool It is a very good library Which helps you in making LLM powered applications So the rest are very practical things When we build different applications We will see all these concepts practically You will be able to appreciate more but since this is the first video I really hope I was able to develop an understanding What is Langchain and why is it required Now let's discuss one more thing What are people using Langchain to make in India? So there are multiple use cases I will give you some examples The first and most popular use case is that you can use Langchain To make conversational chatbots So we are living in the internet era And most of the companies around us are internet based Uber, Zomato, Swiggy are all internet based companies And the biggest problem in internet based companies is the scale Scale means they have to deal with many customers at the same time So we have to talk to customers So one way is to set up your call center Now setting up a call center and hiring a lot of people Is a big challenge What if I have a chatbot Who can talk like a call center executive Who can understand the user's query And provide solutions So my problem is solved as a business So many companies are making chatbots So the first layer of communication Between the company and the customer Is the chatbots And when they are unable to handle the chatbots They forward the query to a human And the human handles it So the most popular use case is that you can use Langchain And you can make chatbots Second use case is AI knowledge assistant So AI knowledge assistant is basically You can think of it as a chatbot But it is trained on your data Or it has access to your data Let's take an example, let's say our campusX website Where our courses are run I want us to integrate a chatbot in our courses So that the student can quickly ask about the doubt So that the student can quickly ask about the doubt So here it is That it is a chatbot but it also knows about my data The lecture is running And the knowledge of the chatbot is there So you can easily build this knowledge assistant With the help of Langchain And we will do it further So agents are basically chatbots on steroids So agents are basically chatbots on steroids So agents are basically chatbots on steroids They can not only talk, they can also do some work Like for example, make my trip What do people do in make my trip? In make my trip, people book hotels Book trains tickets, book flights tickets So generally what happens is that Older people in 60s They are not so fluent in working with such websites Or in booking tickets So what can we do? We can make an agent sit here An AI agent who will not only talk like a human But when you will tell him He will do things for you Like if a senior citizen is talking to this AI agent He will literally say that Book the cheapest flight ticket from this place to this place The cheapest one So this AI agent has these tools and this power That he can execute this whole task by himself It is said that AI agents are the next big thing in the world of AI It is said that AI agents are the next big thing in the world of AI And most likely they will be and the good thing is that You can make agents using Langchain In fact, we will make a basic agent in this playlist So this is also a very interesting use case There can be many other use cases You can do workflow automation Any kind of workflow automation at personal level, professional level, company level You can use LLMs to automate the workflow Hence you can use Langchain Fifth use case is summarization and research helper So what you can do is To simplify research papers or books You can use Langchain to simplify research papers So you already know that You can't upload big books on chat GPT Because of the problem of context length Second, there can be another problem That sometimes you can't upload your company's private data on chat GPT Your company has denied it So what you can do is that your company can use Langchain And make a tool like chat GPT Which can process a big document And can answer questions related to it And second, since this is your company's personal chatbot So you can upload private data related to your company And talk So tools related to summarization and research have also started to be made Using Langchain and apart from this There will be many other tools in the future But the future seems bright So now we will see more LLM based applications Like websites boom, apps boom It seems like LLM based applications boom And Langchain will play a key role I would like to cover one last thing In this introduction video Langchain is not the only framework that helps you in making LLM applications There are multiple other frameworks as well Two of them are very famous One of them is Lama index and the second one is Haystack Both these frameworks are very popular And there are many companies that are using these frameworks Instead of using Langchain It really depends on where you are getting better pricing Which tool you are finding the best But Lama index is a little more popular You might have heard about it, there is a course on it on our website And the second one is Haystack So this is the library and framework And here also You can easily make LLM based applications If you want, I can give you a comparative study Between Langchain, Lama index and Haystack But I feel that since we have not studied Langchain well So it is not the right time But in the future, I will definitely touch this topic Where I will analyze pros and cons between Lama index, Langchain and Haystack But for now, you should know that Langchain is not the only framework There are others as well So with that, I will conclude this video I really hope that with this video I was able to introduce you to Langchain And you have an idea of what is Langchain And why it is needed And what you can do with it Now we are ready to move ahead in our journey In the next two videos, we will discuss the complete ecosystem of Langchain And trust me, it is very important to know that Once we know that Then we will jump into practical parts We will start coding and making LLM based applications So if you liked the video, please do like, share and subscribe See you in the next video, bye!

The first benefit of Langchain is
That there is a concept of chains
In fact, the name of Langchain is Langchain because it is a chain concept
So what you can do in chains is
You can give a chain form to different components
Basically you can form a pipeline structure
And you can execute many complex workflows
Like in our example
You have to execute multiple tasks between multiple components
You have to load the document, split the text
You have to do the embedding, store it in the database
Then you have to do the retrieval and send the data to LLM
Now this is a series of tasks
So you can convert this whole pipeline into a chain
So the best feature of chain is
That the output of one component and the input of the other component
Is automatically made
You don't have to write this code manually
And not only that, you can form very complex chains
You can form sequential chains, parallel chains, conditional chains
So no matter how complex the workflow is
No matter how many tasks there are
You can make that pipeline using chains
So this is the biggest feature that we will see later
The second big feature is
Model agnostic development
Meaning you can use any model
It doesn't matter, I gave you an example
You can use OpenAI or Google
You have to change the code of one line
And it will be converted
You can use AWS or GCP
So you can easily shift the code base
To a different component
So basically this model is agnostic
You have to focus on your core logic, business logic
Move the components there, it doesn't matter
Third is that
Langchain has tried to give you a complete ecosystem
If you want a document loader, then all kinds of document loader
Are available, you can bring the data from the cloud

You can load any kind of thing

You have a lot of databases
So basically every component you use
Has a lot of varieties available
